Understand Fall Clearance Ferrmix Make sure there is enough clearance below you for your fall arrest system to work properly. Consider swing-fall hazards when working away from anchor points. Real-World Reminder Many fall incidents happen within the first few minutes of starting a task because workers rush or assume the area is safe. Slow down, inspect your equipment

Test before you touch – try start, test voltage, release pressure. • Only the person who locked it removes it Near miss- LOTO(Lock out-Tag out) in Temporary Substation , miss communication No shortcuts, no exceptions. Remember about live energies between IDOM and Actemium. • “Assumed dead” is not dead – always test • Temporary power and commissioning phases increase risk • Live work must be authorized, planned, and controlled • If you’re not qualified – don’t touch it Stop & think

• Labels and covers are installed. • No exposed conductors are present. GRK Action plans to be assessed with Stegra HSE and close them out. Respect Lockout/Tagout (LOTO) • Only authorized personnel remove LOTO devices. • Never bypass or remove someone else’s tag. • If in doubt, stop and ask. Safe Positioning & PPE • Stand to the side of panels during energization — not in front. • Wear required PPE: arc-rated clothing, insulated gloves, face protection, and safety boots. 1NM-Inadequate fall protection: 20.04.2026 •During site walk, a scaffolder on the piperack between WTP2 and ELA01 was seen working without anchoring hooks as missing collective protection. •Immediate verbal reminder given to team member #2 on the ground to ensure use of hooks or collective protection and to pass message to scaffolder; agreement confirmed. •Follow-up checks showed scaffolder later walking unanchored again, although collective protection was installed during observation; IDOM and

First Aid 27th April: Pipe bridge priority 3 involving ABAB worker lifting some scaffold material. Material slipped and hit his nose, resulting in a small cut . Assisted at the Emergency center on site getting the first aid needed. Nothing major. He is in good health condition.

NM, 4th May: During Actemium planned inspection to address items raised on the snagging list covering the full length of the Pipe Rack. It was seen that there was damage to multiple areas of the total run of the 33kv cable, from ELA01 to NPB511. Upon investigation it was apparent that the damage had been caused by drilling of cable raceway/containment when fixing labelling. Later reported that there a total of 16 damage point to the 33kv cable. Investigation completed

NM, 15th May: MEWP work was carried out above an unbarricaded area; a dropped spanner was identified, and workers were not tied to approved anchor points. Highlights: • Placing and setting the LNG tanks. All safety measures properly implemented. • Safety induction for contractors launched.

NM, 21st May: Priority 2, zone 2, during lifting operation, a scaffold standard post was struck and damaged by a telehandler during pipe movement. Highlights / Lowlights • Delivery and installation of the 4th LNG tank. All safety measures properly implemented. • Safety induction for contractors launched. Ongoing. • Drill on 18th of May with ERT Stegra: Heart attack –CPR • Signing, barricading, scaffolding. etc. consolidate campaigns!!!!

hse.highlights: NM 26th May: X-ray tests were conducted on the welds of the pipes on the pipe bridge, by Inspector NDT. … ...no exposure, inspectors were arranging the equipment. PTW protocol not followed, RAMS requirements not followed. Failure in communication and official channels were not used to pass this information of the activities planned. No supervision from Ferrmix representatives. Dropped objects: still many safety observations related to this issue. Review campaigns of working at height and dropped

Avenue 6 excavation works: Barrication still needs to be improved with hard barriers close to ASP area. Poor communication and coordination: PDOP area ongoing works . Anfra failure to properly coordinate with Actemium team. Exclusion zone of working area to be improved.

LNG Terminal: 3 pressure tests delivered in a safe manner. Controlled by IDOM, CHART and STEGRA. Celebrating with Contractors HSE and Stegra top management 1-year LTI free for BOP area. Refresh campaigns of WAH and dropped objects. Completed

BOP-IDOM-EHSS – SAFETY MOMENT-Electrical W26 
2
Electrical work during pre-commissioning is one of the highest-risk phases because systems begin transitioning from “under construction” to “partially energized.”
Verify All Circuits Are De-Energized
Before touching any electrical component, confirm the system is isolated and locked out.
Apply LOTO and verify zero energy.
Use an approved voltage tester before starting work.
Treat all cables as live until proven otherwise.
Control Temporary Power Hazards
Temporary power systems are often exposed, overloaded, or poorly protected during construction.
Keep cables elevated or protected from mechanical damage.
Ensure distribution boards are closed and labelled.
Never bypass RCD/GFCI protection.
Maintain Safe Clearance Around Energized Panels
Pre-commissioning often requires energizing panels for testing—keep the area controlled.
Establish a restricted zone around live panels.
Allow only authorized personnel inside the zone.
Use insulated tools and wear arc-rated PPE when required.
Prevent Contact with Damaged Cables
Construction activity can easily damage cables, creating hidden shock or arc hazards.
Inspect cables daily for cuts, crushed insulation, or exposed conductors.
Remove damaged cables from service immediately.
Report hazards to the supervisor before continuing work.
Stay Alert During Pre-Commissioning Tests
Systems may be energized unexpectedly during testing or troubleshooting.
Confirm test schedules with commissioning teams.
Keep clear of equipment under test.
Never assume a system is safe because it was isolated earlier.
Electrical hazards increase significantly during pre-commissioning because systems transition from safe to energized states. The safest crews are the ones who 
verify isolation, control temporary power, and maintain strict boundaries around energized equipment
